title: Audit-as-a-Service contract — fabric-os ↔ repo
principle: fabric-os centralizes audit/report management. Each repo provides a conformant audit/ + reports/ tree; fabric-os indexes fleet state, enforces freshness + honesty coverage, and owns ecosystem rollups. Engine (rubric/runners) stays in bridge-os; coverage denominator stays in canon-os.

vocabulary.principle: Four distinct artifact types — never conflate them. The AUDIT finds what's wrong (assessment); the HANDOFF says what to do next (directive); you REMEDIATE; the REPORT records what you did; EVIDENCE proves it happened.

audit.is: the assessment / evaluation against the standard — what the state is, what is wrong, how it scores
audit.answers: what is the state, what's wrong, how does it score
audit.folder: audit/reports/ (dated assessment write-ups) + audit/evidence/ (the proof behind the scores)

handoff.is: the directive / work-order — the prioritized list of what to do next, synthesized from both lenses (SIGNAL weakest-link first, then MPR threshold gaps by leverage)
handoff.answers: what should I do next, in what order, and how will I know it's done
handoff.folder: audit/handoff/ (dated work-orders; each action: closes <finding> · gate <threshold/level> · owner · evidence-to-produce)

report.is: the remediation record — what you actually DID about the audit findings, before -> after, outcome
report.answers: what we did about it, the result
report.folder: reports/ (dated remediation reports — each cites the evidence proving the work)

evidence.is: proof / verification of audit, handoff, and report — machine artifacts that show the assessment is real and the remediation actually happened
evidence.answers: prove the audit is real and the remediation happened
evidence.folder: audit/evidence/ (witnesses, probe outputs, before/after — current-only, machine)

lenses.principle: Two complementary assessment lenses, NOT merged — coexisting views. Both shape this framework and are applied when running audits + giving remediation feedback.

mpr.shape: 11 pillars x score 0-100; foundational + transformational tiers; thresholds 85 unlock / 95 world-class

signal.shape: 6 dimensions x level L0-L5; weakest-link = overall; production-only scoring; half-levels
signal.blockedOn: XR-AGENT-CAPABILITY-OWNERSHIP-001 (SIGNAL evaluator to be built in baseline-os)

lifecycle.principle: A loop designed to close on itself. At L3 a human kicks each step; at L5 an agent runs it unattended, human approval only at authority Class A/S.

steps.name: remediate
steps.owner: agent (Class R) or repo owner (Class A/S)

steps.name: re-verify
steps.note: handoff item auto-closes when its gate clears

maturityModel.ceiling: SIGNAL L5 across all 6 dimensions / MPR 95+ across all 11 pillars
l5Additions: closed-loop autonomy — agents run the loop unattended (Tooling/Process L5, agenticEmpowerment 95)
predictive monitoring — trend lines, drift detection, breach forecast (Monitoring L5)
adversarial honesty — red-team lens refutes every verdict + signed provenance (Safeguards L5, trustAndSafety 95)
enforced ownership — CODEOWNERS-style binding + SLA + escalation (Team & Ownership L5)

ownership.principle: L5 Team & Ownership (§4c.4): every artifact + handoff item has a codified owner, SLA, and escalation path — machine-checkable via aaas:honesty:ownership, not a doc convention. An artifact past its SLA escalates; a handoff item with no owner is a contract violation.

handoffItem.escalation: P24 to the named owner; unowned item = contract violation

repo.handoffDirective: audit/handoff/handoff-YYYY-MM-DD.md (dated work-order; synthesized from both lenses; each action cites finding/gate/owner/evidence)
repo.remediationReport: reports/<action>-YYYY-MM-DD.md (dated record of what was DONE; cites evidence)
repo.evidenceProof: audit/evidence/<witness>-latest.json (machine proof, current-only — verifies audit + report)

auditProfiles._note: Required witness TYPES (matched by prefix against audit/evidence/<type>-latest.json). Reconciled to the 11-pillar taxonomy: mpr-repo is the composite the bridge-os engine emits (carries all pillars); aaas-honesty-gate is the anti-superficiality witness. Legacy five-pillar/composite/docs-canon retired.

enforcement.note: Contract check is advisory until each repo's pin + reports/ + cadence are provisioned, then per-repo hard-block in CI (mirrors the --closed-specs rollout).
